content.problemStatement:
Chef wants to buy candy for his friend. He went to a store which sells candies from NNN jars, arranged in a circle and numbered 111 through NNN in such a way that for each valid iii, jars iii and i+1i+1i+1 are adjacent, and jars 111 and NNN are also adjacent. For each valid iii, the candies in the iii-th jar have a sweetness AiA_iAi​.

Obviously, Chef wants to buy the sweetest candies. However, he gets attracted by a limited-time offer in the store and wants to use it. The offer is as follows: Chef has to choose an integer kkk (1≤k≤N1 \le k \le N1≤k≤N), then choose kkk jars which are contiguous in the circle, and finally, he can buy the candies from these jars at a discounted price. For a fixed kkk, Chef chooses the kkk contiguous jars uniformly randomly (among all possible sets of kkk contiguous jars), but once he chooses them, he buys only candies with the largest sweetness among the chosen jars.

As you may have guessed, Chef is confused about which value of kkk to choose. For each valid kkk, let's denote the expected value of the sweetness of the candies he will buy if he chooses this value of kkk by SkS_kSk​. Then, let's denote Fk=Sk⋅NF_k = S_k \cdot NFk​=Sk​⋅N; it can be proved that for each valid kkk, FkF_kFk​ is an integer. Can you help Chef find the values of F1,F2,…,FNF_1, F_2, \ldots, F_NF1​,F2​,…,FN​?
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers F1,F2,…,FNF_1, F_2, \ldots, F_NF1​,F2​,…,FN​.
